rulings.Case Title: SHAWN O'LEARY VS. RICHARD ALLGIRE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Vacate Arbitration Mtn To Compel Contractor State License Board To Continue And Complete Investigation Of Deft Previously Started
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 20, 2019, Line 3, PETITIONER SHAWN O'LEARY Motion To Vacate Arbitration; Motion To Compel Contractor State License Board To Continue And Complete Investigation Of Deft Previously Started. Shawn O'Leary's "motion to vacate arbitration" is denied. CCP §1288 provides that a petition to vacate an arbitration award "shall be served and filed not later than 100 days after the date of the service of a signed copy of the award." O'Leary does not contend that he met this deadline; indeed, the arbitration award was issued in February 2018. O'Leary's other requested relief is mooted by the denial of his "motion to vacate arbitration." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for respondent is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: KORAY ERGUR VS. CALIFORNIA MORTGAGE & REALTY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Complaint With Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 20, 2019, Line 16, DEFENDANT RICHARD JOHNSON Amended Motion To Dismiss Complaint With Prejudice. Defendant Richard Johnson's motion to dismiss the complaint with prejudice is granted, as he was not timely served. Plaintiff filed this case against Johnson and others in March 2016, but Johnson was not served until October 2019 - personally at his Menlo Park, California home. CCP §583.210(a) requires that "[t]he summons and complaint shall be served upon a defendant within three years after the action is commenced against the defendant." Dismissal is "mandatory" if the three-year deadline is missed. (Busching v. Sup. Ct. (1974) 12 Cal.3d 44, 50.) It appears plaintiff tried to serve Johnson by mail in March 2016. However, as this court's clerk informed plaintiff: "The Proof of Service of Summons forms…as to defendants Richard A. Johnson and Lloyd Coleman are insufficient. If the manner of service was by Mail and Acknowledgment within the state of California, a completed Notice and Acknowledgment of Receipt [form POS-015] must be attached to the POS-010 form, and must be signed by the actual defendant. The certified mail receipts that are attached to both Proofs of Service would only be valid if the mail was sent to an address outside of California, and the return receipts would also need to be signed by the actual defendants." (See CCP §415.30.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Johnson is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MOTIVA ENTERPRISES LLC A DELAWRE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. AXIS SURPLUS INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Renewed Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Seal Summary Judgment Filings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 20, 2019, Line 18, PLAINTIFF MOTIVA ENTERPRISES LLC Renewed Motion For Order To Seal Summary Judgment Filings. Because Motiva Enterprises LLC has now complied with California Rules of Court 2.550 and 2.551, its motion to seal the summary judgment filings listed on page 2 of its memorandum is granted. I am now able to - and do - make the findings required by CRC 2.550(d). I do not agree with the memo's comments on page 13; the Rules of Court do not provide that any court record is sealed "as a matter of course." (Id.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for Motiva is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 2.550(d) which also repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CUONG VAN TRAN VS. RAMON PARAS-NISCE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 20, 2019, Line 14, Motion To Compel Arbitration. Cuong Van Tran's motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. Tran and Ramon and Natividad Paras-Nisce are required to arbitrate the disputes in the complaint as well as the declaratory relief claim in the cross-complaint based on the broad arbitration clause in their real estate purchase agreement. (Id. at 36.) Strong public policies favor arbitration and there is no proof that the separable agreement to arbitrate was procured by fraud. (Brown v. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 938, 953, 958.) Also, I exercise discretion under CCP §§ 1281.2(d) and 1281.4 to stay court prosecution of the claims against the Re/Max cross-defendants pending the arbitration's resolution. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Tran is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is DENIED. The defendant's response are code compliant. Generally speaking, the requests seek documents from Defendant John Stewart Company reflecting agreements or correspondence between other parties. The Defendant's amended responses specifically state they engaged in a diligent search and were unable to locate any responsive documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSUE LOPEZ VS. JOEL II BALDWIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 20, 2019, Line 8, PLAINTIFF JOSUE LOPEZ'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is DENIED. The plaintiff misreads the requirements of Form Interrogatory No. 15.1. The interrogatory is stated in the alternative - i.e., "state all facts upon which you base the denial or special or affirmative defense. . . " (emphasis added). The defendant's Answer contains affirmative defenses and did not plead any specific denials of allegations. As such, their responses are adequate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
